thought: I have Apple (6.3%→8.0%), Microsoft (13.1%→12.9%), and Alphabet (15.1%→14.9%). Apple is the lowest in intensity but growing fastest. I'll frame this around the insight that Apple's absolute R&D trajectory is steeper than intensity suggests, due to revenue scale.

content:
**Apple R&D Intensity vs. Large-Cap Peers (2019–2023)**

Apple's R&D intensity has grown from 6.3% to 8.0% of revenue — the steepest rate of increase among peers. In absolute terms, R&D spend rose $13.7B (+84%), outpacing revenue growth of 57%.

**Apple R&D Intensity vs. Large-Cap Peers (2019–2023)**

Apple's R&D intensity has grown from 6.3% to 8.0% of revenue — the steepest rate of increase among peers. In absolute terms, R&D spend rose $13.7B (+84%), outpacing revenue growth of 57%.

Microsoft held steady at ~13% intensity throughout the period, with 2023 showing renewed investment in AI infrastructure (Azure OpenAI). Alphabet maintained the highest intensity at ~15%, reflecting sustained investment in Search AI, Cloud, and Other Bets.

**Key insight:** Apple's lower headline intensity (8% vs. 13–15% for peers) understates its competitive position — Apple's R&D is highly concentrated in silicon (M-series, Neural Engine) and a narrower product surface area, suggesting higher output-per-dollar efficiency. The intensity gap is structural, not a sign of underinvestment.

output.summary: Apple's R&D intensity has grown from 6.3% to 8.0% of revenue (2019–2023), the steepest increase among peers. Absolute spend rose $13.7B (+84%). Microsoft held at ~13% intensity; Alphabet at ~15%. Apple's lower intensity reflects structural differences in product scope and silicon concentration — not underinvestment. The intensity gap vs. peers is closing.
